sensitivity.contextMeaning: How well the body reacts to insulin
sensitivity.sentence: Over weeks, regular movement often improves sleep and insulin sensitivity.

minute.simpleMeaning: A very short time, sixty seconds
minute.sentence: Even a 20-minute walk can help some people feel calmer later.

walk.contextMeaning: Going outside on foot for exercise
walk.sentence: Even a 20-minute walk can help some people feel calmer later.

help.sentence: Even a 20-minute walk can help some people feel calmer later.

some.sentence: Even a 20-minute walk can help some people feel calmer later.

people.sentence: Even a 20-minute walk can help some people feel calmer later.

calmer.sentence: Even a 20-minute walk can help some people feel calmer later.

many.simpleMeaning: A large number of things or people
many.sentence: For many women, the menstrual cycle can also change sleep, appetite, and mood.

women.sentence: For many women, the menstrual cycle can also change sleep, appetite, and mood.

menstrual.simpleMeaning: About a woman’s monthly body time
menstrual.contextMeaning: About the monthly body changes that can affect sleep and feelings
menstrual.sentence: For many women, the menstrual cycle can also change sleep, appetite, and mood.

cycle.simpleMeaning: A thing that happens again and again
cycle.contextMeaning: The monthly body changes that happen again and again
cycle.sentence: For many women, the menstrual cycle can also change sleep, appetite, and mood.

appetite.simpleMeaning: How much you want to eat
appetite.contextMeaning: How hungry she feels and how much food she wants
appetite.sentence: For many women, the menstrual cycle can also change sleep, appetite, and mood.

estrogen.simpleMeaning: A body chemical in girls and women
estrogen.contextMeaning: A body chemical that goes up and down during the month
estrogen.sentence: Estrogen and progesterone rise and fall across the month.

progesterone.simpleMeaning: A body chemical in girls and women
progesterone.contextMeaning: Another body chemical that changes during the month
progesterone.sentence: Estrogen and progesterone rise and fall across the month.

fall.contextMeaning: To become lower after being higher
fall.sentence: Estrogen and progesterone rise and fall across the month.

across.simpleMeaning: From one side to the other
across.sentence: Estrogen and progesterone rise and fall across the month.

month.sentence: Estrogen and progesterone rise and fall across the month.

bad.contextMeaning: Feeling worse or having a hard day
bad.sentence: So Shizuka’s “bad day” could be sleep stress plus a normal cycle change.

plus.contextMeaning: Sleep stress and also a normal body change
plus.sentence: So Shizuka’s “bad day” could be sleep stress plus a normal cycle change.

wednesday.contextMeaning: The day Shizuka tried something new
wednesday.sentence: On Wednesday evening, Shizuka tried a small experiment.

experiment.simpleMeaning: Trying something to see what happens
experiment.contextMeaning: A small test to see if something helps
experiment.sentence: On Wednesday evening, Shizuka tried a small experiment.

protein.simpleMeaning: Food that helps your body grow strong
protein.contextMeaning: A breakfast food that helps keep her full and strong
protein.sentence: She ate a simple breakfast with protein.

took.sentence: She took a ten-minute walk outside after waking.

ten-minute.contextMeaning: A walk that lasts ten minutes
ten-minute.sentence: She took a ten-minute walk outside after waking.

drank.simpleMeaning: Took liquid into your mouth and swallowed it
drank.contextMeaning: She took water into her mouth and swallowed it before coffee

water.simpleMeaning: Clear liquid people and animals drink
water.contextMeaning: A clear drink she had before coffee

before.contextMeaning: She had water earlier than coffee

went.simpleMeaning: Moved from one place to another
went.contextMeaning: She moved to bed and slept
went.sentence: She also went to bed at the same time.

bed.simpleMeaning: A soft place where you sleep
bed.contextMeaning: The place she went to sleep in
bed.sentence: She also went to bed at the same time.

same.contextMeaning: At the very same time as something else
same.sentence: She also went to bed at the same time.

still.contextMeaning: The next day, she had work and stress too
still.sentence: The next day, she still had work and stress.

control.simpleMeaning: Being able to manage things well
control.contextMeaning: She felt more able to manage what was happening
control.sentence: But she felt more in control.

lesson.simpleMeaning: Something you learn from a story or event
lesson.contextMeaning: The important thing to learn from this story
lesson.sentence: The lesson is not “one hormone equals one problem.” The lesson is the chain.

equals.contextMeaning: One thing does not always mean one other thing
equals.sentence: The lesson is not “one hormone equals one problem.” The lesson is the chain.

problem.simpleMeaning: Something bad or hard to deal with
problem.contextMeaning: Something bad or hard that happens
problem.sentence: The lesson is not “one hormone equals one problem.” The lesson is the chain.

spot.contextMeaning: If you notice the first change, you can guess the next one
spot.sentence: If you spot the first change, you can often predict the next one.

first.contextMeaning: The one that happens at the beginning
first.sentence: If you spot the first change, you can often predict the next one.

predict.contextMeaning: Guess what will happen next after the first change
predict.sentence: If you spot the first change, you can often predict the next one.

reminder.contextMeaning: A story that helps you remember an important idea
reminder.sentence: Shizuka’s story is a good reminder.

system.simpleMeaning: Many parts work together as one thing
system.contextMeaning: The body works together as one whole, not as separate parts
system.sentence: Your body is one system, not separate boxes.

separate.contextMeaning: Not split into different parts or boxes
separate.sentence: Your body is one system, not separate boxes.

boxes.simpleMeaning: Square containers for keeping things inside
boxes.contextMeaning: Different parts or groups, like things put in different containers
boxes.sentence: Your body is one system, not separate boxes.

each.contextMeaning: Sleep, stress, light, food, and movement send messages to one another
each.sentence: Sleep, stress, light, food, and movement all send messages to each other.

moody.simpleMeaning: Feeling unhappy or changing feelings easily
moody.sentence: When you feel tired, hungry, and moody at the same time, it may be a chain reaction.

reaction.simpleMeaning: Something that happens after something else
reaction.contextMeaning: One feeling or problem causes another feeling or problem
reaction.sentence: When you feel tired, hungry, and moody at the same time, it may be a chain reaction.

try.simpleMeaning: Do your best to do something
try.contextMeaning: Make an effort to ask that question
try.sentence: Try to ask, “What happened first?” That question can guide your next choice.

ask.contextMeaning: Say the words to find out something
ask.sentence: Try to ask, “What happened first?” That question can guide your next choice.

question.simpleMeaning: A sentence that wants an answer
question.contextMeaning: The words about what happened first; it helps you think
question.sentence: Try to ask, “What happened first?” That question can guide your next choice.

guide.simpleMeaning: Help someone know what to do
guide.contextMeaning: Help you decide your next step
guide.sentence: Try to ask, “What happened first?” That question can guide your next choice.

choice.simpleMeaning: One thing you decide to do
choice.contextMeaning: The next thing you decide to do
choice.sentence: Try to ask, “What happened first?” That question can guide your next choice.

practice.simpleMeaning: Doing something again to get better
practice.contextMeaning: Learning English by doing it with real situations
practice.sentence: If you want more English practice with real-life topics, visit ovi english dot com.

real-life.contextMeaning: About things that happen in normal daily life
real-life.sentence: If you want more English practice with real-life topics, visit ovi english dot com.

topics.simpleMeaning: Things people talk or learn about
topics.sentence: If you want more English practice with real-life topics, visit ovi english dot com.

visit.simpleMeaning: Go to see a place or website
visit.sentence: If you want more English practice with real-life topics, visit ovi english dot com.

dot.contextMeaning: The small sign between the website name and ending
dot.sentence: If you want more English practice with real-life topics, visit ovi english dot com.

com.simpleMeaning: A website ending on the internet
com.contextMeaning: The last part of the website address
com.sentence: If you want more English practice with real-life topics, visit ovi english dot com.

opening.contextMeaning: The start of the lesson or video
opening.sentence: [OPENING] Welcome back to Ovi English School.

news.simpleMeaning: New things people tell others about
news.contextMeaning: A story about something that happened
news.sentence: [NEWS STORY] Shizuka stayed up late to finish homework.

why.contextMeaning: Asking for the reason something happened
why.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Why did Shizuka feel worse after two bad nights of sleep?

worse.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Why did Shizuka feel worse after two bad nights of sleep?

nights.simpleMeaning: Times when it is dark and people sleep
nights.contextMeaning: Two dark times when she did not sleep well
nights.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Why did Shizuka feel worse after two bad nights of sleep?

answer.simpleMeaning: What you say to a question
answer.contextMeaning: The reply that explains the reason
answer.sentence: ANSWER: Her body kept stress signals higher, so focus and mood became less steady.

kept.contextMeaning: Made the stress signals stay high
kept.sentence: ANSWER: Her body kept stress signals higher, so focus and mood became less steady.

higher.contextMeaning: At a stronger level than normal
higher.sentence: ANSWER: Her body kept stress signals higher, so focus and mood became less steady.

became.sentence: ANSWER: Her body kept stress signals higher, so focus and mood became less steady.

stay.simpleMeaning: Keep being in one place or way
stay.sentence: ANSWER: It is a stress hormone that helps you wake up and stay alert.

make.contextMeaning: Cause insulin to work less well
make.sentence: ANSWER: It can make insulin work less well, so blood sugar changes can trigger cravings.

trigger.sentence: ANSWER: It can make insulin work less well, so blood sugar changes can trigger cravings.

cravings.simpleMeaning: Strong wants for food or drink
cravings.sentence: ANSWER: It can make insulin work less well, so blood sugar changes can trigger cravings.

sets.simpleMeaning: Makes something start or work in a way
sets.contextMeaning: Makes your body clock work at the right time
sets.sentence: ANSWER: It sets your body clock and helps melatonin rise at a good time at night.

thing.sentence: QUESTION: What is one simple thing Shizuka changed to feel better?

changed.contextMeaning: Did something new instead of the old way
changed.sentence: QUESTION: What is one simple thing Shizuka changed to feel better?

bedtime.simpleMeaning: The time to go to sleep
bedtime.contextMeaning: The regular time she went to sleep
bedtime.sentence: ANSWER: She got outdoor light after waking and kept a more regular bedtime.

closing.simpleMeaning: The last part at the end
closing.contextMeaning: The final part of the story
closing.sentence: [CLOSING] Shizuka’s story is a good reminder.
